walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Island
walkthrough.text: Let's set up Forbidden Island! First, shuffle all twenty-four island tiles with their colorful side face up. Arrange them in a diamond shape: two tiles on the top row, then four, then six, then six, then four, then two on the bottom. This creates the island you will be exploring and trying to escape from.

walkthrough.title: Set the Water Meter
walkthrough.text: Now grab the water meter and its clip. Choose your difficulty level and set the clip accordingly. Novice starts at level one, Normal at level two, Elite at level three, and Legendary at level four. For your first game, we recommend Normal difficulty.

walkthrough.title: Flood the Island
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all twenty-four flood cards into a face-down deck. Draw six flood cards from the top. For each card drawn, find the matching island tile and flip it to its blue flooded side. Place those six flood cards in a face-up discard pile next to the flood deck.

walkthrough.title: Assign Adventurer Roles
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the six adventurer cards and deal one to each player. Each player takes the matching colored pawn. Return unused adventurer cards and pawns to the box. Take a moment to read your special power aloud so everyone knows what each role can do.

walkthrough.title: Place Pawns on Starting Tiles
walkthrough.text: Each player places their pawn on the island tile that matches their role's starting icon. If that tile happens to already be flooded, place your pawn there anyway. The tile is still part of the island until it sinks completely.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Treasure Deck
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the twenty-eight treasure cards into a face-down deck. Deal two cards to each player. If anyone gets a Waters Rise card during this deal, shuffle it back into the deck and deal a replacement. Place the four treasure figurines nearby where everyone can see them.

walkthrough.title: Choose First Player
walkthrough.text: Pick a first player however you like. The rulebook suggests whoever most recently traveled. Play will go clockwise from the first player. Everyone is on the same team, so turn order matters less than in competitive games. Let's save this island!

Set clip on water meter accordingly

Place drawn cards in flood discard pile

summary.title: Choose First Player

Cooperative game — all players on the same team

walkthrough.title: Three Phases Per Turn
walkthrough.text: Each player's turn has three phases done in order. First, you take up to three actions. Second, you draw two treasure cards. Third, you draw flood cards equal to the current water level. Let's walk through each phase in detail.

walkthrough.title: Phase One: Take Actions
walkthrough.text: You get up to three actions on your turn, and you can use them in any combination. Your four choices are: move to an adjacent tile, shore up a flooded tile, give a treasure card to another player on your tile, or capture a treasure. You can repeat actions if you want, and you do not have to use all three.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Move your pawn to an adjacent tile. Adjacent means up, down, left, or right, but not diagonal. You can move onto flooded tiles, but you cannot move onto sunken tiles or empty spaces. The Explorer can also move diagonally, and the Pilot can fly to any tile once per turn.

walkthrough.title: Action: Shore Up
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about shoring up. Pick a flooded tile that is adjacent to you or the tile you are standing on, and flip it back to its normal side. This costs one action per tile. The Engineer gets a special deal and can shore up two tiles for just one action. Shoring up is your main defense against the rising waters.

walkthrough.title: Action: Give a Treasure Card
walkthrough.text: You can hand one of your treasure cards to another player, but you both must be standing on the same tile. The Messenger is the exception and can send cards to any player anywhere on the island. Remember there is a hand limit of five cards, so the receiving player must discard down if they go over.

walkthrough.title: Action: Capture a Treasure
walkthrough.text: This is how you win the game. If you are standing on one of the two tiles associated with a treasure and you have four matching treasure cards in your hand, discard those four cards and take the treasure figurine. You only need one of the two associated tiles to still exist to capture the treasure.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Draw Treasure Cards
walkthrough.text: After your actions, draw two cards from the treasure deck. If you draw a Waters Rise card, resolve it immediately. The water level goes up one notch, and you shuffle the flood discard pile and place it on top of the flood draw pile. This means recently flooded tiles will flood again soon. Waters Rise cards do not go to your hand.

walkthrough.title: Phase Three: Draw Flood Cards
walkthrough.text: Now draw flood cards equal to the current water level on the meter. That is usually two or three cards early on, but can be four or five later. For each card, check the matching tile. If it is normal, flip it to flooded. If it is already flooded, it sinks forever. Remove both the tile and the flood card from the game.

walkthrough.title: Special Cards: Helicopter Lift and Sandbags
walkthrough.text: Two special treasure cards can be played at any time, even on another player's turn. Helicopter Lift moves any number of pawns from one tile to any other tile. Sandbags lets you shore up any tile anywhere on the island without spending an action. Save at least one Helicopter Lift for your final escape.

walkthrough.title: Swimming When a Tile Sinks
walkthrough.text: If a tile sinks while someone is standing on it, that player must immediately swim to an adjacent tile. If there is no adjacent tile to swim to, the entire team loses the game instantly. The Diver can swim through multiple flooded or missing tiles to reach safety, and the Pilot can fly anywhere.

walkthrough.title: How to Win
walkthrough.text: Your team wins when all four treasures have been captured, all players have gathered on Fools' Landing, and someone plays a Helicopter Lift card. That is it. Everyone escapes together and you win as a team.

walkthrough.title: How You Lose
walkthrough.text: You lose immediately if any of these happen. Both tiles for an uncollected treasure sink. Fools' Landing sinks. Any player drowns because they cannot swim to an adjacent tile. Or the water meter reaches the skull and crossbones at the top. Keep an eye on all four of these conditions throughout the game.

summary.title: Three Phases Per Turn
bullets: Phase 1: Take up to 3 actions
Phase 2: Draw 2 treasure cards
Phase 3: Draw flood cards = water level

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Island
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of Forbidden Island! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a few turns with three players. Player 1 is the Engineer, Player 2 is the Explorer, and Player 3 is the Messenger. The island is flooding around you, and you need to work together to grab four treasures and escape. Let's go!

walkthrough.title: Survey the Island
walkthrough.text: Before anyone takes a turn, let's look at the board. Six tiles are already flooded from setup. Identify where the treasure tiles are. Each treasure has two matching tiles. Find Fools' Landing, which is your escape point. Now check which treasure tiles are closest to flooded or in danger.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Engineer's First Turn
walkthrough.text: Player 1, the Engineer, starts near a flooded tile. Let's use the Engineer's special power right away. Shore up the flooded tile you are on and one adjacent flooded tile. That is two shore-ups for just one action! Now use your second action to move toward Temple of the Moon. Save your third action to shore up another tile along the way.

walkthrough.title: Drawing Treasure Cards
walkthrough.text: Player 1 draws two treasure cards. Let's say you get an Earth Stone card and a Crystal of Fire card. Nice start. Check your hand limit. You have two cards total, well under the five card limit. Now draw flood cards equal to the water level and resolve them.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: Explorer Moves Out
walkthrough.text: Player 2, the Explorer, can move diagonally. Use this to cut across the island faster than other players. Move diagonally toward Coral Palace, one of the Ocean's Chalice tiles. Shore up any flooded tile you pass near. The Explorer's diagonal movement is incredibly valuable for reaching distant parts of the island quickly.

walkthrough.title: Player 3: The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 3, the Messenger, decides to move three times toward a treasure tile on the far side of the island. But wait. The Messenger does not need to physically be next to other players to trade cards. Player 3 just wasted three actions moving when they could have used those actions to shore up nearby flooded tiles and traded cards from anywhere on the map.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Use Your Special Power
walkthrough.text: The takeaway here is critical. Each adventurer has a unique power that changes how you should play. The Messenger's power lets them give cards to anyone anywhere. So instead of running across the island to trade, the Messenger should stay central and shore up tiles while sending cards remotely. Always build your strategy around your role's special ability.

walkthrough.title: Waters Rise!
walkthrough.text: During the treasure card draw, Player 2 draws a Waters Rise card. The water level goes up one notch. Now shuffle all the flood discard cards and put them on top of the flood deck. This is devastating because the tiles you just flooded are going to come up again immediately. This is the main tension in the game.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 1, the Engineer, sees a treasure tile that is not in immediate danger and decides to ignore nearby flooded tiles to move toward it. But on the very next flood phase, two of those ignored flooded tiles get their flood cards drawn again. Since they were already flooded, they sink forever! One of them was a treasure tile. Now one treasure is much harder to collect.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Prioritize Shoring Up Treasure Tiles
walkthrough.text: Always shore up treasure tiles and Fools' Landing first. If both tiles for an uncollected treasure sink, you lose the game immediately. Non-essential tiles can be allowed to flood or even sink without consequence. Focus your shoring efforts on the tiles that actually matter for winning.

walkthrough.title: Trading Cards Efficiently
walkthrough.text: Let's see good teamwork in action. Player 3, the Messenger, has collected two Ocean's Chalice cards. Player 2 already has two Ocean's Chalice cards and is near Coral Palace. Player 3 uses one action to send both cards to Player 2 from across the island. Player 2 now has four matching cards and can capture the treasure on their next turn.

walkthrough.title: Capturing the First Treasure
walkthrough.text: Player 2 moves to Coral Palace and discards their four Ocean's Chalice cards to capture the treasure. Take the Ocean's Chalice figurine. That is one down, three to go. Notice how the Messenger's remote trading made this possible much faster than if both players had to physically meet up.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 2 is feeling confident and uses a Helicopter Lift card to zip across the island for fun, just to get closer to the next treasure. But the team only has one Helicopter Lift card left. If they use that one too, they will have no way to escape at the end of the game. Remember, you need a Helicopter Lift card for the final escape from Fools' Landing.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Save a Helicopter Lift
walkthrough.text: Never use your last Helicopter Lift card casually. You absolutely need one to win the game. If all three Helicopter Lift cards are discarded, it becomes impossible to escape. Treat your final Helicopter Lift like a lifeline and only use extras when the tactical benefit is clearly worth it.

walkthrough.title: The Race to Escape
walkthrough.text: The team has now captured three treasures and the island is falling apart fast. The water level is high and four or five flood cards are being drawn each turn. Everyone starts moving toward Fools' Landing while Player 3 hoards the remaining treasure cards to complete the fourth set. Communication is everything right now.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations! You have walked through the core flow of Forbidden Island. You have seen how to use role powers effectively, why shoring up treasure tiles is essential, and why you must save a Helicopter Lift for the escape. Remember, this is a cooperative game. Talk through every decision with your team.

walkthrough.title: Before You Dive In
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to roles, cards, and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

Reference the Appendix → quick-reference for roles and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Protect What Matters
walkthrough.text: Not all tiles are created equal. Focus your shoring efforts on treasure tiles for uncollected treasures and Fools' Landing. These are the only tiles you absolutely need. Non-essential tiles like the Silver Gate or Iron Gate can be left to sink without consequence. Do not waste precious actions saving tiles that do not affect your victory conditions.

walkthrough.title: Trade Cards Early and Often
walkthrough.text: The fastest way to collect four matching cards is to trade with teammates. Decide early which player is collecting which treasure and funnel cards to them. The Messenger is your best friend for this because they can send cards to anyone from anywhere. Do not try to collect all four cards on your own.

walkthrough.title: Leverage Your Role's Power
walkthrough.text: Every adventurer role has a special power that fundamentally changes how they should play. The Engineer should camp near clusters of flooded tiles. The Pilot should handle distant emergencies. The Diver should operate in areas where tiles are sinking. The Navigator should reposition teammates. Build your entire strategy around your ability.

walkthrough.title: Capture Treasures Immediately
walkthrough.text: The moment someone has four matching cards and is near a matching tile, capture that treasure. Do not wait for a more convenient time. Tiles can sink at any moment, and if both tiles for a treasure disappear before you capture it, the game is over. Speed beats optimization in Forbidden Island.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Water Level
walkthrough.text: Each Waters Rise card is a turning point. It raises the water level and shuffles the flood discard pile back onto the deck. This means tiles that just flooded will flood again very soon. When the water level hits four or five, tiles start sinking rapidly. Plan your endgame before the water gets that high.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Here are the biggest beginner mistakes. First, spreading your team across the island instead of working in coordinated pairs. Second, using all your Helicopter Lift cards before the final escape. Third, trying to save every flooded tile instead of focusing on critical ones. Fourth, holding onto five cards and hitting the hand limit right before drawing a treasure you need.

walkthrough.title: Plan the Escape Early
walkthrough.text: Do not forget about the escape. Once you capture your third treasure, start moving everyone toward Fools' Landing. The fourth treasure capture and the Helicopter Lift escape should happen as quickly as possible. Make sure someone is holding a Helicopter Lift card. If Fools' Landing is flooded, shore it up immediately because if it sinks, you lose.

walkthrough.title: Communicate Everything
walkthrough.text: Forbidden Island is a cooperative game and communication is your strongest tool. Share what cards you have. Announce which tiles are in danger. Discuss who should capture which treasure. Plan multiple turns ahead as a team. The island does not care about your feelings, so be direct and strategic in your discussions.

walkthrough.title: Sandbags Timing
walkthrough.text: Sandbags cards can be played at any time, even during the flood phase. The best use is to save a critical tile that just got its second flood card drawn. Instead of losing the tile forever, play Sandbags to shore it up between the flood draws. This reactive play can save the game.

walkthrough.title: Controlling the Flood Deck
walkthrough.text: After a Waters Rise card is drawn, you know exactly which tiles are on top of the flood deck because you just shuffled the discard pile there. Shore up those tiles before they come up again. This is your window to get ahead of the flooding. The tiles in the flood discard pile are always public information, so track them carefully.

walkthrough.title: Role Synergies
walkthrough.text: Certain role combinations are stronger than others. The Messenger and Explorer make a powerful pair. The Messenger trades cards from anywhere while the Explorer covers ground diagonally to reach treasure tiles fast. The Engineer and Navigator work well together because the Navigator can push the Engineer to flooded clusters where their double shore-up is most valuable.

walkthrough.title: Treasure Capture Sequencing
walkthrough.text: Plan which treasures to capture first based on tile danger. If both Temple of the Moon and Temple of the Sun are flooded, prioritize the Earth Stone before those tiles sink. Meanwhile, if Coral Palace and Tidal Palace are both safe, the Ocean's Chalice can wait. Always capture the most endangered treasure first.

walkthrough.title: Positional Efficiency
walkthrough.text: Every action spent moving is an action not spent shoring up or trading. Minimize movement by positioning players in central locations near multiple objectives. The Pilot should act as a firefighter, flying to emergencies while other players work in their neighborhoods. The Diver should operate in the most dangerous sinking zones where other roles cannot safely go.

walkthrough.title: Hand Management
walkthrough.text: The five-card hand limit is a real constraint. If you are holding four different treasure types with no set of four, you are stuck. Plan trades so that one player builds a complete set rather than everyone holding random assortments. Sometimes discarding a card you do not need is better than holding it and blocking your draws.

walkthrough.title: Difficulty Scaling Adjustments
walkthrough.text: On Elite and Legendary difficulty, the game demands near-perfect play. Start shoring up treasure tiles aggressively from turn one. Trade cards on every possible turn. Do not waste a single action on non-essential tiles. The island collapses so fast at high difficulties that you might only have twelve to fifteen turns before the game ends, win or lose.

walkthrough.title: Letting Tiles Go Strategically
walkthrough.text: Once a treasure is captured, its two associated tiles become irrelevant. Let them sink without spending actions to save them. This frees up your shoring efforts for tiles that still matter. Similarly, if a non-essential tile is flooded and far from any player, let it go. Every action counts in the late game.

bullets: Play Sandbags reactively during flood phase to save critical tiles

bullets: After Waters Rise!, you know what's on top of the flood deck

entries.definition: Can move through one or more adjacent flooded or missing tiles for a single action, reaching the nearest reachable tile beyond. Best in sinking zones.
Can shore up two tiles for one action instead of the normal one tile. Extremely valuable for keeping the island intact.
Can move and shore up diagonally in addition to orthogonally. Covers more ground than any other role.
Can give treasure cards to any player regardless of location. Does not need to be on the same tile to trade.
Can move another player up to two adjacent tiles for one action. Great for repositioning teammates.
Once per turn, can fly to any tile on the island for one action. Acts as a firefighter for distant emergencies.
Flip a flooded tile back to its normal side. Costs one action. Can target your current tile or any adjacent tile.
Discard four matching treasure cards while standing on a corresponding treasure tile to claim the treasure figurine.
A tile flipped to its blue side. Still usable for movement and actions but in danger. If flooded again, it sinks.
A tile removed from the game permanently. Cannot be moved onto or through. The flood card is also removed.
A treasure card that raises the water level by one and shuffles the flood discard pile on top of the flood deck. Does not go to your hand.
Move any number of pawns from one tile to any other tile. Playable anytime, even on other players' turns. Required for the final escape.
Shore up any tile anywhere on the island. Playable anytime. Does not cost an action.
The helicopter pad tile. All players must gather here and play a Helicopter Lift card to win. If this tile sinks, the game is lost.
Tracks the current flood level from one to five plus skull. Determines how many flood cards are drawn each turn. Raised by Waters Rise! cards.
Maximum of five treasure cards in hand. If you exceed five after drawing, immediately discard down to five.
When a tile sinks under a player, they must immediately move to an adjacent tile. If no adjacent tile exists, the game is lost.
One of four treasures. Associated tiles: Temple of the Moon, Temple of the Sun.
One of four treasures. Associated tiles: Howling Garden, Whispering Garden.
One of four treasures. Associated tiles: Cave of Embers, Cave of Shadows.
One of four treasures. Associated tiles: Coral Palace, Tidal Palace.
